what_input_feature: A font input that passes enough CMP-distance milestones during GPOS table parsing/shaping to set the GPOS-attachment scratch flag; the true branch is reached only after the buffer accumulates a GPOS attachment, a deep semantic state behind many integer/tag comparisons.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves 9/10 and hits the branch 105 times on average; its CMP_MAP distance-bucket feedback keeps near-match font inputs whose operands approach the gate values, ratcheting toward the kern/GPOS structure that triggers the attachment flag.
why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 (15 hits) because it has only edge feedback and no gradient toward the comparison milestones; value_profile_cmplog blocks 8/10 (6 hits) because adding the per-execution CMP buffer and I2S stage diverts energy without helping reach this structural flag, so it underperforms plain value_profile.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets) preserves inputs that get progressively closer to the comparison values guarding GPOS parsing, a feedback advantage naive lacks and which the extra I2S/CMP-buffer machinery in value_profile_cmplog does not improve.

what_input_feature: A SQL string containing a bracket-quoted identifier whose opening quote char is the literal '[' (0x5b), so that after sqlite3Isquote accepts the quote, the byte tested at line 31402 equals '['. The winner must drive an executable input that reaches Dequote with z[0]=='['.
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner-resolving seed is a valid SELECT statement carrying a bracketed token '[13-3]' — byte 0x5b('[') sits at offset 0x1c and the closing 0x5d(']') at offset 0x21 — so when this token is dequoted, quote=='[' and the true branch is taken (winner hitcount=2 on the branch).
why_loser_doesnt: The loser-blocking seeds are short, malformed fragments (e.g. ';d T 1S', 'T 1; c BINARY') with no '[' opening a quoted token; at the blocker line the losers never satisfy quote=='[' (L side L31402 shows T=0 F=20) and never even parse a valid bracketed identifier, so the equality byte is never produced.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose CMP operands move closer to matching: as havoc mutates bytes toward the syntactically-valid bracketed-SELECT shape, the per-CMP Hamming/prefix-distance buckets register incremental progress and preserve the partial inputs, letting value_profile climb to a parseable '[...]' token. naive lacks CMP_MAP feedback so it gets no gradient signal; in pair 2 value_profile_cmplog additionally has I2S yet still loses, showing the enabling technique is the CMP_MAP gradient (shared by both winner-pairs), not I2S.

source_line: case OP_Le:               /* same as TK_LE, jump, in1, in3 */
what_input_feature: An ASCII SQL statement that parses into a VDBE program containing an OP_Le/OP_Lt/OP_Ge comparison opcode whose two non-NULL operands are evaluated by the integer/MemCompare path, so the comparison case executes its true (jump) side. The winning seed 'SELECT 11-013>=1...' contains explicit relational operators including a '<=' (bytes 3c 3d) and arithmetic that yields comparable integer operands.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile resolves the branch 8/10. Its corpus contains the structurally valid SQL seed 'SELECT 11-013>=1 3=13-3-323=13=13-3-32=1<=13-3-32=...' which carries the '<=' relational operator (W byte 3c('<') at offset 0x27) plus integer operands; the CMP_MAP gradient feedback over the VDBE integer/string comparisons rewards mutants that drive comparison operands closer, building the valid relational expression incrementally.
why_loser_doesnt: naive (loser in pair 1) never resolves (0/10): with only edge-counter feedback it must assemble a valid relational SQL expression by blind havoc/token mutation, its blocking seeds are short garbage like ';d T 1S' and 'T 1; c BINARY' that never parse into an OP_Le comparison opcode. value_profile_cmplog (loser in pair 2) also stalls (1/10) despite having the CMP_MAP gradient, because adding the I2S CmpLog stage and per-execution CMP buffering dilutes its corpus and per-exec budget here rather than helping reach this comparison opcode.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets over intercepted CMPs) provides progress feedback toward forming a comparable relational expression that naive's plain edge counters cannot sense; the I2S CmpLog technique that distinguishes vpc from vp does not help and the vp-only arm wins, so the enabling technique is the CMP_MAP gradient, not I2S.

what_input_feature: Bytes must parse into a SQL expression containing a relational comparison operator (TK_LE/TK_LT/TK_GE/TK_GT family, e.g. ASCII '<=' or '>=' between numeric operands), so the parsed Expr->op lands on the TK_LE case of the switch in resolveExprStep rather than on TK_ID/TK_VARIABLE/junk arms.
why_winner_satisfies: The winning value_profile seed is 'SELECT 11-013>=13...' packed with '>=', '<=', and '=' comparison operators over numeric operands, forming valid comparison expressions whose Expr->op reaches the TK_LE/TK_GE switch arm (T=1 hits on the blocker line under W).
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are degenerate 'SELECT +:SELECT...' / 'SELECT +LLLLLT...' fragments with no well-formed relational comparison; their expressions resolve down the TK_ID / TK_VARIABLE arms (L hits L98354 TK_ID T=5, L98632 TK_VARIABLE T=9) and never the TK_LE arm (L T=0 at L98662).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose intercepted integer/string CMP operands move closer to matching the parser's keyword/operator-token comparisons, giving partial-progress credit that lets it assemble a valid comparison expression. Both decisive pairs have value_profile as winner: it beats naive (which lacks CMP_MAP) and beats value_profile_cmplog (where adding the I2S CmpLog stage regresses, so the win is attributable to the CMP_MAP gradient, not I2S).

what_input_feature: A SELECT query whose expression body uses a varied mix of relational/arithmetic operators (=, >=, <=, -, /) at offsets 0x06-0x2d, so that an integer comparison inside sqlite3ExprCodeTarget produces a near-matching operand pair (the rare true branch at line 103533, T=1/F=62 in W).
why_winner_satisfies: The value_profile winner seed is 'SELECT 11-013>=1 3=13-3-323=13=13 ...' with distinct single-byte operators per offset (3e(>),3d(=),2d(-),2f(/),3c(<)), exactly the operand-distance gradient that CMP_MAP rewards; W resolves 9/10 and is the only side that takes the blocker true branch (W T=1 F=62 vs L T=0 F=743).
why_loser_doesnt: Both losers (naive, value_profile_cmplog) stay blocked: their inputs are degenerate digit/operator runs ('SELECT 222&22222...', '2222222222222222') concentrated on '2'(0x32), '&'(0x26) and '1'(0x31) across offsets 0x07-0x2d, never assembling the operator diversity that nudges the CMP operand pair across the threshold; naive has no CMP feedback at all and value_profile_cmplog's added substitution stage diverts mutation effort away from the gradient.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient: each intercepted integer CMP writes a (PC, operand-distance bucket) entry, so an input producing operands closer to matching adds a new bucket and is preserved, walking the corpus toward the rare true branch. naive lacks this feedback entirely; value_profile_cmplog adds a substitution stage that here is counterproductive (it loses to plain value_profile), so the enabling technique is the CMP_MAP gradient.
